title: useEffect Dependencies
description: Understand when useEffect re-runs based on dependencies.
question: You have: useEffect(() => { fetchTasks(filter); }, [filter]). When does fetchTasks get called?
options: Only once when the component mounts
On every render of the component
On mount and whenever the filter value changes
Never, because fetchTasks is not in the dependency array
explanation: A useEffect with [filter] in the dependency array runs its callback on initial mount and again whenever the filter value changes. An empty dependency array [] would run only on mount, no array at all would run on every render, and fetchTasks does not need to be in the array since only its argument (filter) determines when to re-fetch.
